title: Diya W.F.C.

infobox.value: ([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): دیا ویمین فٹبال کلب), also referred to as **Diya WFC**, **Diya FC**, **Diya**, **Diya Women FC**, or **DWFC**, is a Pakistani [women's association football](/wiki/Women%27s_association_football "Women's association football") and [futsal](/wiki/Futsal "Futsal") club based in [Karachi](/wiki/Karachi "Karachi"). *Diya* means [lamp](/wiki/Diya_(lamp) "Diya (lamp)") in [Urdu](/wiki/Urdu "Urdu"). It is the country's oldest women's football club,[[1]](#cite_note-Balochistan_United_look_to_transform-1) established in 2002. Diya WFC has no affiliation to any men's club.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Diya W.F.C.](/w/index.php?title=Diya_W.F.C.&redirect=no "Diya W.F.C."))

Pakistani football club established in 2002

Football club

Diya Women Football Club  
دیا ویمین فٹبال کلب

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Full name | Diya Women Football Club |
| Short name | Diya WFC |
| Founded | 2002; 24 years ago (2002) |
| Ground | Clifton Community Ground, [Karachi](/wiki/Karachi "Karachi") |
| Manager | Sadia Sheikh |
| League | [National Women Football Championship](/wiki/National_Women%27s_Football_Championship_(Pakistan) "National Women's Football Championship (Pakistan)") |
| [2021](/wiki/2021_National_Women_Football_Championship "2021 National Women Football Championship") | Quarterfinals (season cancelled) |
| Website | <https://www.diyawfc.org.pk/> |
|  | |
| |  | | --- | | **[Home colours](/wiki/Kit_(association_football) "Kit (association football)")** | | |
|  | |

**Diya Women Football Club** ([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): دیا ویمین فٹبال کلب), also referred to as **Diya WFC**, **Diya FC**, **Diya**, **Diya Women FC**, or **DWFC**, is a Pakistani [women's association football](/wiki/Women%27s_association_football "Women's association football") and [futsal](/wiki/Futsal "Futsal") club based in [Karachi](/wiki/Karachi "Karachi"). *Diya* means [lamp](/wiki/Diya_(lamp) "Diya (lamp)") in [Urdu](/wiki/Urdu "Urdu"). It is the country's oldest women's football club,[[1]](#cite_note-Balochistan_United_look_to_transform-1) established in 2002. Diya WFC has no affiliation to any men's club.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diya_WFC&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Diya WFC was founded in 2002 by Sadia Sheikh, a [physical education](/wiki/Physical_education "Physical education") teacher,[[2]](#cite_note-2) founder and secretary general of [Sindh Women Football Association](/w/index.php?title=Sindh_Women_Football_Association&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sindh Women Football Association (page does not exist)") since 2006 [[3]](#cite_note-3) and first [FIFA](/wiki/FIFA "FIFA")-certified football administrator in Pakistan, in 2011,[[4]](#cite_note-Feature_:_Empowering_women_through_football-4) and the only one so far.[[5]](#cite_note-The_Unstoppables!-5) It was recognized by the [Pakistan Football Federation](/wiki/Pakistan_Football_Federation "Pakistan Football Federation") (PFF) in 2006.[[4]](#cite_note-Feature_:_Empowering_women_through_football-4) Since 2010, Diya WFC is a permanent member of the PFF committee, among 29 members.[[6]](#cite_note-6) In September 2015, Diya WFC was the first women's football club to sign an agreement with Aspirations Pakistan Football Alliance (APFA) and follow their certified curriculum to improve standards of training for both [coaches](/wiki/Coach_(sport)#Association_football "Coach (sport)") and [players](/wiki/Football_player "Football player").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

[Trainings take place on a ground](/wiki/Training_ground_(association_football) "Training ground (association football)") located in [Clifton](/wiki/Clifton,_Karachi "Clifton, Karachi"),[[8]](#cite_note-8) next to [Bagh Ibne Qasim](/wiki/Bagh_Ibne_Qasim "Bagh Ibne Qasim") beachside park. Like other independent women's clubs in Pakistan, Diya WFC has neither [sponsorship](/wiki/Sponsor_(commercial) "Sponsor (commercial)") nor affiliation to a corporation or a [public administration](/wiki/Public_administration "Public administration"), and is therefore financially supported by the owner, family and friends.[[1]](#cite_note-Balochistan_United_look_to_transform-1)[[5]](#cite_note-The_Unstoppables!-5)

Diya WFC is also a competition organizer, such as the 1st Chase Up All-Karachi [Under 23](/wiki/Under-23_sport "Under-23 sport") Girls Futsal Tournament 2021, held in February 2021 in partnership with the [Sindh Women Futsal Association](/w/index.php?title=Sindh_Women_Futsal_Association&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sindh Women Futsal Association (page does not exist)").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Beside women's football, Diya WFC is a football promoter, offering [mixed gender practice](/wiki/Mixed-sex_sports "Mixed-sex sports")[[10]](#cite_note-Diya_Academy's_U11_side-10) or setting up [academies for children](/wiki/Youth_system "Youth system") across [Sindh](/wiki/Sindh "Sindh").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

In 2011, Diya WFC was featured for its pioneer role in a documentary produced by the [UNICEF](/wiki/UNICEF "UNICEF").[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Players

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diya_WFC&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Players")]

As of July 2017, 2,000 girls and women have been trained by Diya.[[5]](#cite_note-The_Unstoppables!-5)

In 2018, students and players age range was from 6 to 27.[[13]](#cite_note-France_football-13)

### Present players

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diya_WFC&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Present players")]

[Kiran Qureshi](/w/index.php?title=Kiran_Qureshi&action=edit&redlink=1 "Kiran Qureshi (page does not exist)") is the only Diya's player to be a member of the [Pakistan women's national football team](/wiki/Pakistan_women%27s_national_football_team "Pakistan women's national football team") (PWNFT), as attacker, since 2020.[[14]](#cite_note-PFF_teams-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)

[Shumaila Gulab Hussain](/w/index.php?title=Shumaila_Gulab_Hussain&action=edit&redlink=1 "Shumaila Gulab Hussain (page does not exist)"), [Marium Zehri](/w/index.php?title=Marium_Zehri&action=edit&redlink=1 "Marium Zehri (page does not exist)") and [Zunaira Shah](/w/index.php?title=Zunaira_Shah&action=edit&redlink=1 "Zunaira Shah (page does not exist)") played for the PWNFT and represented their country at the [South Asian Football Federation](/wiki/South_Asian_Football_Federation "South Asian Football Federation") and [Asian Football Confederation](/wiki/Asian_Football_Confederation "Asian Football Confederation") events in [Bhutan](/wiki/Bhutan "Bhutan") and [Mongolia](/wiki/Mongolia "Mongolia") in 2018.[[16]](#cite_note-Feature_:_Futsal_is_for_girls-16) Shumaila Gulab Hussain and Marium Zehri are [defenders](/wiki/Defender_(association_football) "Defender (association football)") within the under-16 PWNFT.[[14]](#cite_note-PFF_teams-14)

### Past players

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diya_WFC&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Past players")]

[Misha Dawood](/w/index.php?title=Misha_Dawood&action=edit&redlink=1 "Misha Dawood (page does not exist)") was a 19-year-old Diya WFC [midfielder](/wiki/Midfielder "Midfielder") and a second year [UC Berkeley](/wiki/University_of_California,_Berkeley "University of California, Berkeley") student who died in [a plane crash on 28 July 2010](/wiki/Airblue_Flight_202 "Airblue Flight 202"). She was considered a "promising star".[[17]](#cite_note-17) On 4 August 2010, the best woman player award of the [National Women Football Championship](/wiki/National_Women%27s_Football_Championship_(Pakistan) "National Women's Football Championship (Pakistan)") was renamed in her honor by the [PFF](/wiki/Pakistan_Football_Federation "Pakistan Football Federation").[[18]](#cite_note-18) The [National Women Under-16 Championship](/w/index.php?title=Shahlyla_Baloch_National_Women_Under-16_Championship&action=edit&redlink=1 "Shahlyla Baloch National Women Under-16 Championship (page does not exist)") was also renamed in her honor.

[Hajra Khan](/wiki/Hajra_Khan_(footballer) "Hajra Khan (footballer)"), [forward](/wiki/Forward_(association_football) "Forward (association football)") and [captain](/wiki/Captain_(association_football) "Captain (association football)") of PWNFT, played with Diya as a professional from 2008 to 2014, prior to moving to [Balochistan United W.F.C.](/wiki/Balochistan_United_WFC "Balochistan United WFC")[[1]](#cite_note-Balochistan_United_look_to_transform-1) [Mehwish Khan](/wiki/Mehwish_Khan "Mehwish Khan"), also playing as [defender](/wiki/Defender_(association_football) "Defender (association football)") for the PWNFT, started playing with Diya WFC[[19]](#cite_note-19) prior to joining WAPDA women's club in 2011. Along with Hajra Khan and Mehwish Khan, [Marium Leghari](/w/index.php?title=Marium_Leghari&action=edit&redlink=1 "Marium Leghari (page does not exist)"), a Diya player, represented Pakistan in the first [women's South Asian Football Federation Championship](/wiki/2010_SAFF_Women%27s_Championship "2010 SAFF Women's Championship") held in [Bangladesh](/wiki/Bangladesh "Bangladesh") in 2010.[[20]](#cite_note-Squad_named_for_women's_SAFF_Championship-20) [Rukhsar Rashid](/w/index.php?title=Rukhsar_Rashid&action=edit&redlink=1 "Rukhsar Rashid (page does not exist)"), who joined Diya WFC in 2009 and is now coach of the club,[[4]](#cite_note-Feature_:_Empowering_women_through_football-4) was also a defender with the national team. Among the women players who have been part of the national team, [Afshan Altaf](/w/index.php?title=Afshan_Altaf&action=edit&redlink=1 "Afshan Altaf (page does not exist)"),[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22) [Qurratulain Ashraf](/w/index.php?title=Qurratulain_Ashraf&action=edit&redlink=1 "Qurratulain Ashraf (page does not exist)") and [Fatima Ansari](/w/index.php?title=Fatima_Ansari&action=edit&redlink=1 "Fatima Ansari (page does not exist)")[[4]](#cite_note-Feature_:_Empowering_women_through_football-4) started playing with Diya WFC.

[Manizeh Zainli](/wiki/Manizeh_Zainli "Manizeh Zainli"), secretary general of PFF (2020), and the first women to hold this position, played football with Diya WFC for two years as an [amateur](/wiki/Amateur_sports#Association_football "Amateur sports").[[23]](#cite_note-23)

## Performance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diya_WFC&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Performance")]

### Football

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diya_WFC&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Football")]

#### PFF competitions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diya_WFC&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: PFF competitions")]

The club competes within three PFF championships: under-16, under-19 and open age:[[24]](#cite_note-DWFC_History-24)

* [National Women Football Championship](/wiki/National_Women%27s_Football_Championship_(Pakistan) "National Women's Football Championship (Pakistan)")

Diya WFC was ranked 2nd in [2009](/wiki/2009_National_Women_Football_Championship "2009 National Women Football Championship") and [2011](/wiki/2011_National_Women_Football_Championship "2011 National Women Football Championship"), 3rd in [2007](/wiki/2007_National_Women_Football_Championship "2007 National Women Football Championship") and 4th in [2014](/wiki/2014_National_Women_Football_Championship "2014 National Women Football Championship").[[25]](#cite_note-25)[[24]](#cite_note-DWFC_History-24) Hajra Khan won the Misha Dawood Trophy in [2010](/wiki/2010_National_Women_Football_Championship "2010 National Women Football Championship").[[20]](#cite_note-Squad_named_for_women's_SAFF_Championship-20)

* [Shahlyla Baloch National Under-16 Women Football Championship](/w/index.php?title=Shahlyla_Baloch_National_Women_Under-16_Championship&action=edit&redlink=1 "Shahlyla Baloch National Women Under-16 Championship (page does not exist)")

It earned the 3rd position in 2015,[[26]](#cite_note-26) in 2018[[27]](#cite_note-27) and in 2019.[[28]](#cite_note-28) In 2014, the club was rewarded with the [fair play](/wiki/Sportsmanship "Sportsmanship") trophy and prize.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

#### Other competitions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diya_WFC&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Other competitions")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9e/Diya_Women_Football_Club_-_competitions_abroad.png/330px-Diya_Women_Football_Club_-_competitions_abroad.png)](/wiki/File:Diya_Women_Football_Club_-_competitions_abroad.png)

Black = Pakistan  
Blue = Countries where Diya WFC has taken part in an international competition

The [State Bank of Pakistan](/wiki/State_Bank_of_Pakistan "State Bank of Pakistan") held its own women's championship, in which Diya WFC earned the 3rd place during the 4th edition in 2016.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

#### International youth friendly competitions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diya_WFC&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: International youth friendly competitions")]

Sponsored by [Geo News](/wiki/Geo_News "Geo News") of [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") and Alma Culture Center of [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"), and with the personal financial support of Diya's managers, U-13 and U-14 teams went to Norway in July 2017 to participate to the [Norway Cup](/wiki/Norway_Cup "Norway Cup"), without scoring any goal and getting 23 against.[[31]](#cite_note-Pakistani_girls_to_participate-31)[[32]](#cite_note-Une_équipe_pakistanaise-32)[[33]](#cite_note-33)[[34]](#cite_note-Diya_Women_junior_teams-34) It was the first time a Pakistani team participated in a European tournament.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

In August 2017, Diya WFC's G-16 team went to [Turkey](/wiki/Turkey "Turkey") to play friendly matches, sponsored by the [Turkish Cooperation and Coordination Agency](/wiki/Turkish_Cooperation_and_Coordination_Agency "Turkish Cooperation and Coordination Agency"), and U-13 and U-14 teams went to [China](/wiki/China "China") for the [Gothia Cup](/wiki/Gothia_Cup#Gothia_Cup_China "Gothia Cup").[[31]](#cite_note-Pakistani_girls_to_participate-31)[[32]](#cite_note-Une_équipe_pakistanaise-32)[[34]](#cite_note-Diya_Women_junior_teams-34) In August 2019, a mixed gender U-11 team went also to China for the Gothia Cup[[10]](#cite_note-Diya_Academy's_U11_side-10) and won the fair play trophy among 66 participating teams.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

### Variations of football

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diya_WFC&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Variations of football")]

#### Futsal

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diya_WFC&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Futsal")]

The club won the All-Pakistan Women Futsal Championship 2010, defeating Eco Adventure Club Islamabad (1–0), and [Fatima Ansari](/w/index.php?title=Fatima_Ansari&action=edit&redlink=1 "Fatima Ansari (page does not exist)") and [Kiran Yousuf](/w/index.php?title=Kiran_Yousuf&action=edit&redlink=1 "Kiran Yousuf (page does not exist)") were declared best players of the tournament.[[37]](#cite_note-37)

In 2021, Diya WFC won the All Karachi Under-23 Girls Futsal Tournament, among eight teams, defeating Karachi Ladies FC (8–2), and Shumaila Gulab Hussain was declared as one of the best players.[[38]](#cite_note-38)[[16]](#cite_note-Feature_:_Futsal_is_for_girls-16)

#### Five and six-a-side football

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diya_WFC&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Five and six-a-side football")]

Diya WFC also participates to [Leisure Leagues](/wiki/Leisure_Leagues "Leisure Leagues") events in Pakistan. It earned the Leisure Leagues Women Football's Pink Cup 2020, held in October to promote [breast cancer awareness](/wiki/Breast_Cancer_Awareness_Month "Breast Cancer Awareness Month"), defeating Garrison Football Academy (2–0).[[39]](#cite_note-39) The club won the Leisure Leagues Women's Cup 2021, defeating IBA United (2–0).[[40]](#cite_note-40)

## Social activities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Diya_WFC&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Social activities")]

Through sport, Diya WFC's purpose is to give girls a chance for [empowerment](/wiki/Women%27s_empowerment "Women's empowerment"), and *"transport, education and health fees are entirely covered"* by the club[[13]](#cite_note-France_football-13)[[41]](#cite_note-United_Nations_Pakistan-41) It also teaches the players soft skills.[[5]](#cite_note-The_Unstoppables!-5)

Since 2008, every 25 February, Diya WFC and the [United Nations](/wiki/United_Nations "United Nations") hold a friendly match with girls from poor areas of Karachi to promote [gender equality](/wiki/Gender_equality "Gender equality"), [equal rights](/wiki/Equality_before_the_law "Equality before the law") and to stop [violence against women](/wiki/Violence_against_women "Violence against women").[[42]](#cite_note-42)[[41]](#cite_note-United_Nations_Pakistan-41)

For the [International Women's Day](/wiki/International_Women%27s_Day "International Women's Day") of 2015, the PFF held for the first time an [Asian Football Confederation](/wiki/Asian_Football_Confederation "Asian Football Confederation") workshop in collaboration with Diya WFC and [Balochistan United W.F.C.](/wiki/Balochistan_United_WFC "Balochistan United WFC")[[43]](#cite_note-43)

In June 2018, Diya WFC, in collaboration with [Right To Play](/wiki/Right_To_Play "Right To Play") and Women Win, at the invitation of Thar Foundation, organized a football discovery session (including a four-month preparation, training of coaches and matches) and provided [equipment](/wiki/Kit_(association_football) "Kit (association football)") for U-13 girls in the [remote](/wiki/Extremes_on_Earth#Remoteness "Extremes on Earth") district of [Tharparkar](/wiki/Tharparkar "Tharparkar"),[[44]](#cite_note-44)[[45]](#cite_note-45)[[46]](#cite_note-46) which has the lowest [Human Development Index](/wiki/Human_Development_Index "Human Development Index")[[47]](#cite_note-47)[[48]](#cite_note-48) [of Pakistan](/wiki/Districts_of_Pakistan "Districts of Pakistan"). A similar operation was conducted in September 2020 in different areas of Karachi, along with Right To Play, Women Win and the [US mission in Pakistan](/wiki/Embassy_of_the_United_States,_Islamabad "Embassy of the United States, Islamabad").[[49]](#cite_note-49)

In October 2020, Imam Baloch, director of the [National Bank of Pakistan](/wiki/National_Bank_of_Pakistan "National Bank of Pakistan"), lauded Diya WFC's actions for spreading football to poor and "backward areas", such as [Karachi](/wiki/Karachi "Karachi") localities of [Malir](/wiki/Malir_Subdivision "Malir Subdivision"), [Lyari](/wiki/Lyari "Lyari") and [Ibrahim Hyderi](/wiki/Ibrahim_Hyderi "Ibrahim Hyderi") and such [Sindh province districts](/wiki/List_of_districts_in_Sindh "List of districts in Sindh") of [Hyderabad](/wiki/Hyderabad_District,_Sindh "Hyderabad District, Sindh"), [Thatta](/wiki/Thatta_District "Thatta District"), [Mirpur Khas](/wiki/Mirpur_Khas_District "Mirpur Khas District") and [Tharparkar](/wiki/Tharparkar "Tharparkar"), as well as discovering new talents over there.[[50]](#cite_note-50) In [Thatta](/wiki/Thatta "Thatta"), the club organized a four-day camp for 10 to 16 years old girls and awareness workshops about food and nutrient and [COVID-19](/wiki/COVID-19 "COVID-19").[[51]](#cite_note-51)
